reason: 1D SPECIALIZATION FALLS OUTSIDE COAREA SCOPE. lecA1.tex Corollary (eq. coarea-1d) specializes to n = m = 1, which is the case of equal dimensions. But Federer 3.2.12 requires m > n (strict inequality in Federer's notation), i.e., domain dimension strictly greater than codomain dimension. When both are 1, this condition fails. The 1D result IS correct mathematically (it is the elementary substitution formula), but it cannot be derived as a 'specialization' of Federer 3.2.12. It should instead cite the change-of-variables formula or the area formula (Federer 3.2.11). The proof of Theorem thm:equivalence (Step 2) therefore does not actually use the coarea formula at all -- it uses the elementary substitution rule, which the addendum correctly identifies in Remark rem:delta-composition but incorrectly packages as a consequence of the coarea formula.
